metadata.name: Bake into a Pie destroys creature, creates Food token, Food ability gains 3 life
metadata.description: p1 casts Bake into a Pie ({2}{B}{B}) targeting p2's 2/2 creature (Bladetusk Boar). Spell resolves: creature is destroyed and moves to p2's graveyard (CR 701.7a), and p1 receives a Food token (CR 111.10b: colorless Food artifact with '{2}, {T}, Sacrifice: You gain 3 life'). p1 then activates the Food token's non-mana ability (ability_index 0) by paying {2} generic and tapping and sacrificing the token (CR 602.2c: sacrifice is paid as a cost). The Food ability goes on the stack (not a mana ability — CR 605.1a: it does not add mana to the mana pool). After both players pass priority, the ability resolves and p1 gains 3 life (40 → 43). Final state: p1 at 43 life, Bladetusk Boar in p2's graveyard, Food token sacrificed and gone from battlefield.
metadata.generation_notes: Bake into a Pie oracle text (verified via MCP lookup_card): 'Destroy target creature. Create a Food token.' at cost {2}{B}{B}. The Food token's activated ability ('{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this token: You gain 3 life.') is defined in food_token_spec() in card_definition.rs lines 815-845 with: requires_tap: true, mana_cost: { generic: 2 }, sacrifice_self: true, effect: GainLife { player: Controller, amount: Fixed(3) }. This is NOT a mana ability (CR 605.1a: does not add mana to pool, so it uses the stack — CR 602.2). The cost breakdown: {2} generic (mana_paid: { colorless: 2 }) + tap + sacrifice. Sacrifice is paid as a cost at activation time before the ability is placed on the stack (CR 602.2c), so the Food token is in the graveyard immediately after activation (not after resolution). The token then ceases to exist as a SBA (CR 111.7 / 704.5d: tokens in non-battlefield zones cease to exist). p1 needs {2}{B}{B} to cast Bake into a Pie (2 generic + 2 black) and {2} to activate the Food token. Bake into a Pie is NOT in the engine's CardDefinition registry (definitions.rs) — this script has a dispute (step 0, action 1) noting the missing definition. The Food token spec itself IS implemented. mana_paid for Bake into a Pie: { colorless: 2, black: 2 }. ability_index 0 is the Food token's sole non-mana activated ability per the activated_abilities vec (index 0-based, mana abilities excluded). NOTE: This script will fail harness validation (CommandRejected) until Bake into a Pie is added to definitions.rs.

disputes.description: Bake into a Pie does not have a CardDefinition in crates/engine/src/cards/definitions.rs. The card must be added before this script can pass harness validation. The Food token spec (food_token_spec) is already defined in card_definition.rs. To add Bake into a Pie: Instant {2}{B}{B}, effects: [DestroyObject { target: DeclaredTarget(0) }, CreateToken { spec: food_token_spec(1) }], targets: [TargetRequirement::TargetCreature]. This is a missing card definition issue, not a rules logic error.

script.step_note: p1 casts Bake into a Pie targeting p2's Bladetusk Boar

actions.description: Initial: Bake into a Pie in p1's hand, Bladetusk Boar on p2's battlefield, p1 at 40 life
actions.note: p1 has {2}{B}{B} in mana pool ready to cast Bake into a Pie
CR 601.2: p1 casts Bake into a Pie ({2}{B}{B}), targeting p2's Bladetusk Boar. Spell moves from hand to stack.

actions.description: After casting: Bake into a Pie on stack, hand empty
actions.note: Bake into a Pie is on the stack targeting Bladetusk Boar
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — top of stack resolves.

resolution.note: CR 701.7a: Bladetusk Boar is destroyed — moved from battlefield to p2's graveyard.
CR 111.10b: Creates one colorless Food artifact token with '{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this token: You gain 3 life.' Token enters battlefield under p1's control (CR 111.1, 111.5).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Instant resolves; both effects execute in order; Bake into a Pie moves to p1's graveyard.

actions.description: After Bake into a Pie resolves: Bladetusk Boar in p2 graveyard, Food token on p1's battlefield, Bake into a Pie in p1's graveyard
actions.note: Bladetusk Boar destroyed (CR 701.7a). Food token created on p1's side (CR 111.10b). Bake into a Pie in p1's graveyard after normal spell resolution.

script.step_note: p1 activates the Food token's non-mana ability to gain 3 life

actions.note: CR 602.2: p1 activates Food token's ability 0 ('{2}, {T}, Sacrifice this token: You gain 3 life.'). Cost paid: {2} generic + tap + sacrifice. Sacrifice is a cost (CR 602.2c): Food token moves to graveyard before ability is placed on stack. This is NOT a mana ability (CR 605.1a: does not add mana) — ability uses the stack.

actions.description: After activation: Food token sacrificed (in graveyard), Food ability on stack
actions.note: CR 602.2c: Sacrifice paid as cost — Food token already in graveyard. Ability on stack.
CR 116.3: Both players pass priority — Food ability resolves.

resolution.note: CR 118.2: p1's life total increases by 3 (40 → 43).

actions.note: CR 608.2: Activated ability resolves; GainLife effect executes.
CR 111.7 / 704.5d: A token in a zone other than the battlefield ceases to exist as a state-based action. The Food token was sacrificed as a cost and is now in the graveyard.
CR 704.3: SBAs checked whenever a player would receive priority. Food token in graveyard ceases to exist.

actions.description: Final state: p1 at 43 life, Food token gone, Bladetusk Boar in p2 graveyard, stack empty
actions.note: p1 gained 3 life from Food activation (40 → 43, CR 118.2). Food token ceased to exist (CR 704.5d). Bladetusk Boar destroyed by Bake into a Pie (CR 701.7a). p2's creature gone, p1 battlefield empty.
